ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Seed Technologists are responsible for ensuring the quality and purity of seeds used in agriculture. They work in seed production, processing, and testing.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Seed Technologists are involved in various activities such as seed production, processing, testing, and quality control. They work in laboratories, seed production units, and other related settings.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.Sc in Agriculture or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture can pursue a B.Sc in Agriculture or related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a postgraduate diploma or certification in seed technology.

future_builder.description: Seed Technologists with experience can advance to senior roles or specialize in specific areas like seed production or quality control.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience as a Seed Technologist

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can switch to seed technology by acquiring relevant skills and certifications.

progression.senior_level: Senior Seed Technologist or Seed Production Officer (5+ yrs)
